steps.title: Identify the two expressions to add
steps.content:
The problem asks for the sum of two polynomials:
  • First expression: $3x^4 + 2x^3$
  • Second expression: $4x^4 + 7x^3$
We need to add: $(3x^4 + 2x^3) + (4x^4 + 7x^3)$

steps.title: Combine like terms (same exponent)
steps.content:
To add polynomials, group terms with the same power of $x$:
$x^4$ terms: $3x^4 + 4x^4 = (3 + 4)x^4 = 7x^4$
$x^3$ terms: $2x^3 + 7x^3 = (2 + 7)x^3 = 9x^3$

steps.title: Write the final sum
steps.content:
Combining all terms:
$$7x^4 + 9x^3$$
The x-cubed coefficient is $2 + 7 = 9$, which matches choice D. That matches choice D — D is correct.
